findings.claim_text: Bringing a new drug to approval costs about $2.6B (DiMasi 2016) and pharma R&D efficiency fell ~80-fold since 1950 (Eroom's Law), so the economic problem AI targets is real and severe.
findings.contradicting_quote: the estimated median capitalized research and development cost per product was $985 million... and the mean was $1.3 billion

findings.claim_text: Headline AI-pharma deal values are ~95-98% contingent biobucks: Recursion's Roche/Genentech deal was announced at up to $12B but had paid only $213M as of November 2025.
AI-discovered molecules show ~80-90% Phase 1 success but revert to ~40% in Phase 2, comparable to historic industry averages, i.e. no demonstrated efficacy advantage.
